title: Permit Declarant - Shipping & Documentation
description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Established in 1972, Pancom Produce Pte Ltd has emerged as one of the leading fresh produce suppliers in Singapore. The company set out with one supreme objective in mind – to source and distribute premium fruits and vegetables produce from the best international growers and cater to all major supermarkets and wholesalers in Singapore. With over fifty years of operation and vast experience in the fresh produce industry, Pancom Produce Pte Ltd has established itself as a robust company that delivers the finest quality fresh produce. Along with great innovations and steadfast commitments to meet the ever-changing industry, Pancom stays committed to ensure all products are “Naturally Fresh & Clean”. Today, Pancom holds a respectable reputation in the fresh produce industry and has garnered strong relationships with a worldwide network of renowned growers and exporters to ensure a wide selection of fresh produce supplies. Pancom Produce Pte Ltd has grown in leaps and bounds over the past years and has ready plans to expand its current operations for the future. Whether you are a grower, an exporter or looking for an innovative fresh supplier, Pancom Produce Pte Ltd is keen to cultivate a dynamic partnership with you to fulfil this exciting vision!

Job Summary

You will manage import and export permit declarations and oversee shipping documentation to ensure seamless shipment processing. Collaborate effectively with overseas suppliers and shipping lines to resolve claims and discrepancies, driving operational efficiency and compliance.

Responsibilities

  • Declare import and export permits accurately using E2open TradeNet to ensure regulatory compliance
  • Manage and organize shipping documentation to support timely shipment processing
  • Liaise proactively with overseas suppliers and shipping lines to coordinate shipment schedules and resolve issues
  • Investigate and handle shipping and trucking claims, ensuring timely resolution and accurate data entry
  • Draft clear and professional emails to Customs for enquiries or to address discrepancies
  • Collaborate with sales, suppliers, shipping companies, and hauliers to facilitate smooth logistics operations
  • Work independently to learn and adapt to evolving customs and shipping procedures
  • Perform additional duties as assigned to support business needs

Required competencies and certifications

  • Possess Certificate for Singapore Customs Competency Test for Declarants (mandatory for customs declaration duties)
  • Minimum of 2 years’ experience in customs declaration and shipping operations, with demonstrated ability to apply knowledge on the job
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel to manage shipping data and documentation effectively
  • Strong interpersonal skills to liaise with multiple stakeholders including sales teams, suppliers, and logistics partners
  • Ability to write professional emails addressing customs enquiries and shipping claims

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Ability to communicate in Mandarin and write Chinese to facilitate liaison with Chinese suppliers (linked to business communication needs)
  • Some accounting knowledge to support financial aspects of shipping claims and documentation
  • Minimum education level of Polytechnic diploma or “A” levels

Other Information

  • Working Hours: 9am to 6pm, Monday to Friday, 5-day week
  • Salary Range: $3,200.00 to $3,500.00 depending on qualification and working experience

Only shortlisted candidates will be notified
